title: We’re Hiring: Freelance IT Sales & Business Development Executive/International Business Development Executive
desc:
Company: The Coder Root
Industry: IT Services | Web Development | App Development | CRM Solutions
Work Type: Freelance / Remote
Compensation: Commission / Project-Based (Negotiable)

The Coder Root is looking for an experienced and result-driven Freelance IT Sales & Business Development Executive who can help us generate quality leads and bring new projects from both Indian and international markets.

Key Responsibilities:
• Generate qualified leads for website development, web applications, mobile apps, CRM, and custom software development projects.
• Find potential clients through LinkedIn, freelance platforms, cold emailing, networking, and other effective channels.
• Connect and communicate professionally with international clients.
• Understand client requirements and coordinate with our technical team.
• Schedule client meetings and actively follow up with prospects.
• Assist in proposal preparation, negotiation, and closing deals.
• Build long-term relationships with potential clients and businesses.

Required Skills:
• Experience in IT Sales, Business Development, or Lead Generation.
• Good understanding of website and software development services.
• Experience in international client acquisition is highly preferred.
• Strong English communication and negotiation skills.
• Ability to independently generate leads and convert opportunities into projects.
• Experience with LinkedIn outreach, cold emailing, and freelance platforms is an advantage.

title: AVEVA R2 HMI System Enhancement
desc:
Industrial HMI Development – AVEVA Edge R2

We are looking for an experienced AVEVA Edge R2 (formerly InduSoft Web Studio) developer to build the complete HMI for an advanced industrial process monitoring and control application.

This is not a graphic design project.

The objective is to transform an existing PowerPoint concept into a fully functional industrial HMI, including dynamic process visualization, navigation, trends, alarms, historical data, and process simulation.

The interface will run on an industrial touch panel PC and must be developed following modern industrial HMI standards.

A PowerPoint presentation and technical documentation describing the process and required functionalities will be provided.

Required Skills
AVEVA Edge R2 / InduSoft Web Studio
Industrial HMI Development
SCADA Systems
Industrial Automation
PLC Communication (future integration)
Dynamic Tags
Alarm Management
Trend Charts
Historical Data
Industrial UI/UX
Scope of Work

The application will include:

Home Dashboard
Multi-Equipment Overview
Individual Equipment Pages
Dynamic Process Visualization
Real-Time Process Values
Interactive Trend Charts
Historical Data
Alarm Management
Historical Alarm Log
Navigation System
Settings Pages
Responsive Touch Interface
Dynamic Animations
Complete Simulation Mode (without PLC connection)
Functional Requirements

The project must include:

Fully dynamic screens
Real-time value simulation
Process animations
Running timers
Dynamic status indicators
Alarm

title: Casual Fashion Copywriting & Reviews
desc:
I’m launching a new line of casual fashion for adults and I need crisp, engaging copy that makes each piece irresistible while sounding authentic and relatable. For every item I will supply basic specs, fabric details, images, and any unique selling points; you will turn that raw data into a polished product description plus a short, review-style blurb that reads like it came from a real customer.

The tone should be confident but conversational—think lifestyle magazine rather than hard-sell advertising—highlighting fit, comfort, and styling versatility. Because these are everyday casual pieces, focus on how they slot seamlessly into an adult’s work-from-home, weekend, or city-errand routine.

Deliverables for each product
• 1 product description (around 120–150 words)  
• 1 persuasive customer-style review (60–80 words)

All copy must be original and SEO-friendly without keyword stuffing. Fluency in fashion terminology is essential, and light research to sprinkle in fabric benefits or care tips is appreciated. I will review the first two items before you continue with the rest to ensure tone alignment.

title: Business Asset Sale Contract Review
desc:
I have a draft contract for the sale of our business assets and I need a sharp legal eye to make sure everything is watertight before we sign. The agreement covers a full transfer of our website, customer information, documented business processes, all business-related IP, and our CRM system.  

Your task is to comb through the document, flag any clauses that expose us to unnecessary risk, and suggest precise wording improvements that protect our interests in areas such as representations & warranties, indemnities, IP assignment, data-protection obligations, and post-completion support.  

You’ll return:  
• A marked-up version with tracked changes or inline comments showing every recommended edit.  
• A concise memo summarising key risks, missing protections, and any follow-up questions you need answered to finalise the agreement.  

Experience with asset purchase agreements and business-asset transfers is essential; familiarity with data privacy and IP law will make your feedback even more valuable. If you can turn this around quickly while keeping the language commercially practical, let’s get started.

title: Automated Job Application AI Agent
desc:
I need an AI-driven agent that searches LinkedIn, Indeed, Glassdoor, and Naukri for roles that match my profile, then completes each application for me end-to-end. The system will pull only my current Resume/CV, but it must still tailor every submission—adapting answers, keywords, and any free-text fields so each application speaks directly to that specific job post.

Core workflow
• Detect fresh, relevant openings on the four platforms in real time or on a scheduled cycle.  
• Parse job requirements, match them against my skills and experience, and decide whether to proceed.  
• Auto-populate the application forms with the right resume variant, contact details, and contextualised responses.  
• Log every action (job link, date, status) so I can review progress from a simple dashboard or export.

Acceptance criteria
• A runnable script or containerised solution (Python preferred, Selenium/Playwright or official APIs where available).  
• Platform-specific modules that respect each site’s TOS and handle captchas, rate limits, and multi-factor prompts gracefully.  
• Config files where I can update search keywords and tweak the tailoring rules without touching the core code.  
• Clear setup documentation plus a short video walkthrough showing the agent submitting at least one live application on each platform.

With these pieces in place I’ll be able to let the agent handle routine job submissions while I focus on interview prep.

title: Hindi School Exam Paper Writer
desc:
I am assembling a fresh bank of Hindi exam questions for school-level learners and need a writer who can create complete, well-structured papers that genuinely test comprehension, grammar, vocabulary, and creative expression.  

Here’s what I’m looking for:  
•  Two full-length papers per class (Classes 6-10), each balanced across multiple-choice, short-answer, and long-answer sections.  
•  Clear marking schemes and model answers for every question.  
•  Difficulty that progresses logically from Class 6 to Class 10, aligned with typical CBSE/State-board expectations.  
•  Original content only—no re-used or AI-translated passages. I will run random plagiarism checks.  

I will review one sample section first; once approved, we will proceed with the remaining sets. If you have prior experience setting Hindi question papers or teaching at the school level, I’d love to see a brief example.

title: After Effects Template Customisation for Product-Launch
desc:
I’ve already licensed an Adobe After Effects template for our upcoming product-launch video. All animations, transitions, and overall timing are locked; what I need now is a clean swap-in of our own material so the piece feels unmistakably on brand.

Here’s what you will receive from me the moment we start:

• High-resolution logo files
• A curated set of product images (PNG/JPEG)
• Final text copy for every frame
• Brand guidelines covering colour values, safe-zone rules, and typography

Your task is to drop each asset into the corresponding placeholder, match the colour palette throughout, and double-check that text sizes, kerning, and animation cues remain balanced once the new content is in place. No structural changes to the template are required; this is purely about precise integration and polish.

Deliverables
1. Rendered video (H.264 MP4, 1080p) ready for immediate upload to our launch pages
2. The updated .aep project folder with all linked footage and fonts collected

Acceptance criteria
• All brand colours match the supplied hex/RGB values
• Logos remain crisp at full resolution with no unintended scaling
• Product images fit frames without stretching or pixelation
• Text replaces the placeholder copy exactly, with grammar and line breaks preserved
• Final render runs smoothly from start to finish with zero dropped frames or artefacts

The template is lightweight, so an experienced After Effects editor should have no trouble hitting a quick turnaround once assets

title: Finish .NET Core Microservices
desc:
I’m in the closing stretch of a .NET Core microservice project and need an extra set of experienced hands to get us over the line. The architecture is already in place and running in containers, but several new endpoints and service refinements still have to be coded, tested, and deployed.

Your focus will be pure development inside the existing Dot net Core microservices: writing clean C# for REST endpoints and message-bus consumers, extending EF Core queries, and ensuring every change sails through our Git- and Jenkins-based CI/CD pipeline. Once the code is committed you’ll containerise it, update the Helm charts, trigger the build, and keep an eye on Kibana to confirm the logs are healthy. Gatling drives our load tests, so any change must survive that suite before we call it done.

We’re already at the final testing and deployment stage, so the pace is quick and feedback is rapid. I’ll assign tasks via Jira, review pull requests in GitHub, and meet briefly over Teams each day to keep momentum high.

Acceptance criteria
• New feature passes unit tests, integration tests, and Gatling load run (p95 < 200 ms).  
• Jenkins pipeline completes without manual intervention and all stages are green.  
• No new warnings or errors appear in Kibana for 24 h on staging.  
• Code is documented with XML comments and any relevant README updates.

If you’re fluent in .NET Core microservices and comfortable owning code from ticket to production, send over a sample repo or snippet that shows

title: One-Month Law Internships Certificate
desc:
I am a current law student and I’d like to secure a genuine, one-month virtual internship with a licensed lawyer, law chamber, or firm. The placement can be in any branch—corporate, criminal, civil, contract, IP, family, or a combination—so long as the work is authentic and the supervising attorney can issue an official internship completion certificate on firm or chamber letterhead.

Alongside me, four to five classmates will join the same program, each needing an individual certificate dated for the same month. We are available to assist with real research, drafting, case summaries, client-memo preparation, or other day-to-day tasks you routinely delegate to interns; this is not meant to be a “paper-only” arrangement.

Key points you should know  
• Format: 100 % virtual (email, Zoom, Google Meet, MS Teams—whatever you already use).  
• Duration: 4 consecutive weeks; flexible start date in the coming month.  
• Supervision: must be a qualified lawyer registered with the local bar, willing to confirm our participation if our university requests verification.  
• Deliverable: a signed and stamped internship certificate for each student upon successful completion.

If you have the capacity to mentor multiple interns remotely and are comfortable providing that formal certification, please outline:  
1. Your practice area(s) and typical intern tasks.  
2. The digital tools you prefer (e.g., LexisNexis, Westlaw, MS Office, Google Workspace).  
3. The daily or weekly time commitme

title: Abishek sathsara Facebook Ads Scaling Specialist
desc:
My personalized-gifting brand, Gifting Smile, is ready to push well beyond its current sales pace through Facebook advertising only. I need a specialist who can plan, launch, and optimise campaigns with one core objective: profitable sales growth.  

You will build the entire funnel—from audience research and pixel / Conversion API set-up through creative direction, testing, and daily bid adjustments—so I can focus on product and fulfilment. We do not have predefined segments; your expertise in constructing look-alikes, interest clusters, and retargeting loops will be critical.

Strong e-commerce results are a must, and experience with gifting, fashion, or other D2C ranges is a welcome bonus. Please come prepared to show past dashboards, case studies, or screenshots that prove you can scale while improving ROAS. Clear, proactive communication matters to me: concise performance updates as you optimise are expected, with a fuller written report each week.

Deliverables  
• Campaign blueprint and audience strategy before launch  
• Live Facebook ad account fully configured (pixel & CAPI verified)  
• Ongoing daily optimisation focused on lowering cost-per-purchase  
• Weekly performance report with next-step recommendations  

I see this as a long-term collaboration, so consistent profitability and transparent results will earn continued work and higher budgets.
